title: PR Curve and Average Precision
description:
Implement the Precision-Recall curve and Average Precision (AP).

AP is the area under the PR curve computed as:
`AP = sum_k (R[k] - R[k-1]) * P[k]`

where detections are sorted by score descending.

**Example:**
```
y_true   = [0, 0, 1, 1]
y_scores = [0.1, 0.4, 0.35, 0.8]
=> AP = 0.8333
```
constraints: Include precision=1, recall=0 at the start
starter_code:
import numpy as np

def pr_curve_ap(y_true: np.ndarray, y_scores: np.ndarray) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, float]:
    """Compute PR curve and Average Precision.

    Returns:
        (precision, recall, ap)
    """
    pass
solution_code:
import numpy as np

def pr_curve_ap(y_true: np.ndarray, y_scores: np.ndarray) -> tuple[np.ndarray, np.ndarray, float]:
    y_true = np.asarray(y_true)
    y_scores = np.asarray(y_scores)
    order = np.argsort(y_scores)[::-1]
    y_true_sorted = y_true[order]
    P_total = np.sum(y_true)
    precisions, recalls = [1.0], [0.0]
    tp, fp = 0, 0
    for label in y_true_sorted:
        if label == 1:
            tp += 1
        else:
            fp += 1
        prec = tp / (tp + fp)
        rec = tp / P_total if P_total > 0 else 0.0
        precisions.append(prec)
        recalls.append(rec)
    precisions = np.array(precisions)
    recalls = np.array(recalls)
    ap = float(np.sum((recalls[1:] - recalls[:-1]) * precisions[1:]))
    return precisions, recalls, ap
explanation: The PR curve is preferred over ROC when the dataset is highly imbalanced. AP summarizes the curve as a single number: the weighted sum of precisions at each recall threshold, with the weight being the increase in recall. Higher AP means better retrieval performance across all thresholds.

test_cases.name: Perfect AP=1
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
_, _, ap = pr_curve_ap(np.array([0,0,1,1]), np.array([0.1,0.2,0.8,0.9]))
assert abs(ap - 1.0) < 1e-9

test_cases.name: AP in [0,1]
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
_, _, ap = pr_curve_ap(np.array([0,0,1,1]), np.array([0.1,0.4,0.35,0.8]))
assert 0.0 <= ap <= 1.0

test_cases.name: Starts at recall=0 precision=1
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
prec, rec, _ = pr_curve_ap(np.array([0,1,1]), np.array([0.2,0.7,0.9]))
assert rec[0] == 0.0 and prec[0] == 1.0

test_cases.name: All positives ranked first gives AP=1
test_cases.code:
import numpy as np
_, _, ap = pr_curve_ap(np.array([1,1,0,0]), np.array([0.9,0.8,0.3,0.1]))
assert abs(ap - 1.0) < 1e-9

hints: Sort by score descending then iterate accumulating TP/FP
AP = sum of delta_recall * precision at each step
